title: Zhongqi Wuliang Unveils Server-Rack Neutral-Atom Quantum Computer at WAIC 2026 - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Chinese startup Zhongqi Wuliang unveiled Qinghe No. 1, a neutral-atom quantum computer fitting into a single standard data-center server rack, the third commercially positioned Chinese neutral-atom system in under a year, though it has not yet published peer-reviewed qubit counts, gate fidelities, or coherence times.

insights.claim: Zhongqi Wuliang unveiled Qinghe No. 1, a neutral-atom quantum computer engineered to fit directly into a standard data-center server rack, spun out of CAS SIOM and founded by Lu Xudong.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A single-rack quantum form factor targets the enterprise datacenter deployment gap, a distinct commercialization vector to track.
insights.quote: a neutral-atom quantum computer engineered to fit directly into a standard data-center server rack

insights.claim: Neutral-atom processors cool atoms to micro-kelvin temperatures internally via laser pulses, eliminating the room-scale liquid-helium cooling loops that superconducting systems require.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The elimination of dilution refrigeration is the core structural advantage letting quantum hardware fit in ordinary data centers.
insights.quote: eliminating the need for external, room-scale liquid-helium cooling loops

insights.claim: Standard superconducting architectures like IBM's and Google's require dilution refrigerators to cool circuits to ~15 millikelvin, demanding specialized infrastructure and vibration isolation that traditional enterprise data centers cannot accommodate.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Frames superconducting's cryogenic burden as a deployment liability that neutral-atom vendors can exploit for datacenter penetration.
insights.quote: This cryogenic equipment demands specialized infrastructure, high energy overhead, and heavy vibration isolation that traditional enterprise data centers cannot accommodate.
insights.locator: Bypassing Dilution Refrigeration for Data-Center Racks

insights.claim: Hardware integration was accelerated via supply chain partner Xuanxiang Technology, which commercialized a metasurface chip that generates large-scale optical tweezer arrays to simplify optical alignment.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Metasurface optics as an enabling component points to a picks-and-shovels supplier layer forming around neutral-atom miniaturization.
insights.quote: a metasurface chip capable of generating large-scale optical tweezer arrays to simplify optical alignment
insights.locator: The hardware integration was accelerated through supply chain partner Xuanxiang Technology

insights.claim: Qinghe No. 1 is the third commercially positioned neutral-atom system to emerge from China's regional quantum hardware cluster in less than a year, following Hanyuan-1 (Oct 2025) and Hanyuan-2 (May 2026).
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Three commercial systems in under a year signals China is iterating neutral-atom hardware faster than most Western timelines assume.
insights.quote: the third commercially positioned neutral-atom system to emerge from China’s regional quantum hardware cluster in less than a year

insights.claim: Hanyuan-1, a 100-qubit room-temperature neutral-atom system by Zhongke Kuyuan occupying three racks, was delivered to a China Mobile subsidiary and exported to Pakistan for its national quantum computing center.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: China exporting quantum hardware to Pakistan's national center marks quantum entering geopolitical tech-diplomacy, a strategic signal.
insights.quote: It was delivered to a China Mobile subsidiary and exported to Pakistan for its national quantum computing center.

insights.claim: Hanyuan-2 is a dual-core cabinet-style system operating below 7 kW of power consumption, designed for parallel processing or main-core/auxiliary-core logical qubit encoding.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Sub-7kW power draw makes these systems datacenter-compatible on power budgets, reinforcing the enterprise-deployment thesis.

insights.claim: The rapid hardware iteration is backed by Shanghai municipal industrial policy that designated neutral-atom technology a core roadmap and established dedicated innovation zones like Xuhui Cultivation Zone and Zhangjiang Quantum Bay with state funds and subsidies.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: State-directed subsidies explain the iteration pace and suggest China's neutral-atom lead is policy-durable, not a one-off.
insights.quote: The city has designated neutral-atom technology as a core roadmap, establishing dedicated innovation zones
insights.locator: This rapid hardware iteration is backed by municipal industrial policies in Shanghai.

themes.theme: Verification Gaps and Global Neutral-Atom Momentum

insights.claim: Zhongqi Wuliang has not yet published peer-reviewed metrics for Qinghe No. 1 such as independent qubit counts, two-qubit gate fidelities, or coherence times, making third-party benchmarks essential.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The absence of verified performance metrics is a red flag; the form factor is an engineering concept until benchmarked against QuEra's systems.
insights.quote: Zhongqi Wuliang has not yet published peer-reviewed metrics for Qinghe No. 1, such as independent qubit counts, two-qubit gate fidelities, or coherence times.

insights.claim: Google Quantum AI launched a dedicated neutral-atom research program in Boulder, Colorado in early 2026 to run alongside its superconducting efforts, citing the modality's reconfigurable connectivity and scalability.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A superconducting incumbent like Google hedging into neutral atoms validates the modality and signals broader architectural realignment.
insights.quote: Google Quantum AI launched a dedicated neutral-atom research program in Boulder, Colorado, to run alongside its superconducting efforts, citing the modality’s reconfigurable connectivity and scalability
insights.locator: The announcement also comes as global interest in neutral atoms expands.
